title: How World War II Transformed Higher Education
subtitle: 二战后大学为何迅速扩张？精英教育走向大众

paragraphs.en: World War II marked a structural turning point for higher education in Western societies. Before the war, university participation in many countries remained a small elite share. Postwar reconstruction and social mobility demands created sustained pressure for far more graduates.
paragraphs.zh: 第二次世界大战是西方高等教育结构性转折的关键节点。战前，许多国家的大学入学率仅局限于少数精英群体。战后重建与社会流动的诉求，对培养更多毕业生形成了持续的压力。

paragraphs.en: Access expanded beyond traditional upper classes and reached groups that had rarely imagined entering universities. From the 1960s onward, many systems grew at extraordinary speed, sometimes doubling enrollment within a decade. Growth, however, remained uneven across institutions and regions.
paragraphs.zh: 入学机会逐渐超出传统上层阶层，覆盖到那些过去几乎不敢想象能上大学的人群。自20世纪60年代起，许多高等教育体系以惊人的速度扩张，有些甚至在十年内实现了入学人数翻倍。不过，这种增长在不同院校和地区之间并不均衡。

paragraphs.en: Rapid expansion brought governance, staffing, and academic culture pressures. Departments that hired large waves of young scholars quickly often saw their norms rewritten in a few years. The lasting challenge became how to combine mass access with quality and institutional coherence.
paragraphs.zh: 高速扩张带来了治理、师资和学术文化等方面的压力。那些在短期内大量聘用青年学者的院系，其内部规范往往在数年内便被重写。此后的长期挑战，便是如何将大众化入学与教学质量及制度连贯性结合起来。
